event: electrocardiogram (ECG) changes suggesting postero-lateral myocardial infarction
evaluated and accepted for resuscitation with tMCS
CentriMag™ Circulatory Support System introduced via left femoral artery and right femoral vein
cardiorespiratory collapse reversed with tMCS (12 min, 3.5 L/min blood flow)
coronary angiogram showed severe 3-vessel coronary artery disease
stents placed in two obtuse marginal arteries
unsuitable for further percutaneous coronary or surgical intervention
tMCS weaning protocol suggested satisfactory recovery after 5 days
tMCS device explanted after further 8 days
arterial cannula re-sited in left subclavian artery via tube graft
venous drainage via right femoral vein remained
decision to perform LVAD implantation (bridge to transplantation)
referred for transplantation in another country
echocardiogram detected ascending aortic intimal flap 12 days into third tMCS period
type A aortic dissection confirmed by CT aortogram
intimal flap extended from ascending aorta to iliac arteries with no communication between true and false lumens
agreed strategy: ascending aorta repair followed by LVAD implantation
ascending aorta dilated with hematoma and free pericardial blood at surgery
cardiopulmonary bypass established via left subclavian cannula and right atrial cannula
ascending aorta replaced with tube graft
distal ascending aorta supported with Teflon strips
myocardial protection with antegrade cold blood cardioplegia and topical slush
HeartMate 3 LVAD implanted via left ventricular apex
outflow graft anastomosed to aortic interposition graft
intensive care unit stay for 2 weeks
no communication between true and false lumens
acute type A aortic dissection with high mortality
